records.ety: [F. dnoncer, OF. denoncier, fr. L. denuntiare, denunciare; de- + nunciare, nuntiare, to announce, report, nuntius a messenger, message. See Nuncio, and cf. Denunciate.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Denounced (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Denouncing.]

defs: To make known in a solemn or official manner; to declare; to proclaim (especially an evil).
quotes: I denounce unto you this day, that ye shall surely perish.

defs: To proclaim in a threatening manner; to threaten by some outward sign or expression.
To point out as deserving of reprehension or punishment, etc.; to accuse in a threatening manner; to invoke censure upon; to stigmatize.
quotes: To denounce the immoralities of Julius Cæsar.
